macro_context.headline: US-Iran ceasefire optimism drives risk-on rally; S&P 500 closes at record 7,365 while WTI tumbles from $110 highs and DXY breaks lower.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD jumped 47 pips to 1.1772, a fresh five-week high, as the dollar broke down across the board on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes pulling the safe-haven bid out of DXY.
EURUSD.why: The euro is the cleanest non-dollar long right now: Bund yields sticky, Lagarde has shut the door on more cuts, and any de-escalation tape sells dollars reflexively.
EURUSD.watch: 1.1800 is immediate resistance, then 1.1850. A formal US-Iran announcement clears both. 11:00 TRT EZ retail sales is the only data risk on the calendar.

headlines.title: Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes
Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
Eurozone inflation hits 3% as oil prices spike and economic growth slows

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Cable rose 52 pips to 1.3620 on the broad dollar slump but lagged EUR (+0.48%) — UK local-election fiscal jitters kept the pound the laggard among majors.
GBPUSD.why: Sterling is caught: a softer dollar wants to push it higher, but markets are pricing in Labour-government polling weakness as a 2027-budget overhang on Gilts.
GBPUSD.watch: BoE's Bailey speaks 14:30 TRT. A hawkish lean on services CPI sticking at 4.7% clears 1.3700; a dovish tone leaves GBP a clear EUR underperformer.

headlines.title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY tumbled 132 pips to 156.36 — its sharpest one-day drop in three weeks — as Iran-deal headlines collapsed the geopolitical risk premium that had been propping the dollar.
USDJPY.why: The pair was the cleanest expression of long-dollar-on-Mideast-tail-risk. With that tail thinning, BoJ-Fed divergence alone cannot sustain 158+ when DXY itself is breaking down.
USDJPY.watch: 155.00 is the line where MoF Kanda's verbal-intervention rhetoric escalates. Tokyo CPI Friday determines whether BoJ has cover for a June hike.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD ripped 0.77% to 0.7260, within 25 pips of its 52-week high, on the broad ceasefire-driven dollar slump and iron ore back above $115/t.
AUDUSD.why: Aussie is the highest-beta DXY-down trade in G10. Softer USD, China stimulus headlines, and copper at multi-year highs is the carry-trade trifecta in one tape.
AUDUSD.watch: 0.7280 is the breakout level; clearance opens 0.7400 fast. Powell speaks 21:00 TRT — any dovish nuance and AUD makes new highs by Asia open.

headlines.title: Sterling Could Fall if U.K. Local Elections Reignite Fiscal Concerns
Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?

XAUUSD.name: Gold (Spot)
XAUUSD.past: Gold added $58 to $4,740/oz on the same Iran-deal flow that crushed oil — counterintuitive, but spot caught a tailwind from the falling dollar (DXY -0.6%) and persistent central-bank bid.
XAUUSD.why: The buy-gold-on-geopolitics trade should have unwound; that it didn't tells you central banks and Asian retail are still the marginal buyer, not Western tactical longs.
XAUUSD.watch: $4,800 is the recent high. If Powell sounds dovish at 21:00 TRT and DXY breaks 97.50, gold prints new ATHs by London open. $4,650 is the support that must hold.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures edge up on hopes for US-Iran deal
Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal
Arizona Eagle reports results from Eagle project drilling

XAGUSD.name: Silver (Spot)
XAGUSD.past: Silver exploded +4.31% to $80.13/oz — dwarfing gold's 1.25% — classic late-cycle melt-up where the laggard catches up on industrial demand and short-covering.
XAGUSD.why: Gold/silver ratio compressed sharply today. This is what end-of-cycle metals rallies look like — silver always overshoots both ways, with solar build-out and China stimulus keeping the bid.
XAGUSD.watch: $82.50 is the immediate resistance and the level that triggers more momentum buying. A failure here and silver round-trips fast — moves are 3-4x gold's.

headlines.title: Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal
Gold and silver prices today, Wednesday, May 6: Gold and silver prices up significantly this morning
Arizona Eagle reports results from Eagle project drilling

USOIL.name: WTI Crude Oil
USOIL.past: WTI cratered $2.10 to $92.97/bbl after a violent round-trip from $110 intra-week, as Iran-deal headlines collapsed the war premium that had been keeping the curve in steep backwardation.
USOIL.why: This is the un-pricing of the Strait of Hormuz tail. With OPEC+ already adding 411k bpd in May and US shale uncapped, no war premium leaves only soft fundamentals.
USOIL.watch: $90 is the technical pivot — failure opens $84 (200-DMA). DOE inventories Wednesday 17:30 TRT; a build above 1.5M accelerates the slide.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures edge up on hopes for US-Iran deal
US gas prices rise above $4.50 as reports point to US-Iran peace deal
3 charts explain how the Iran war oil shock could impact the economy

US500.name: S&P 500 Index
US500.past: S&P 500 closed at a record 7,365.12 (+1.46%), led by tech as Iran-deal headlines triggered a beta-up grab-up across cyclicals and AI names.
US500.why: Textbook macro-tail-risk-removed plus earnings still beating setup. With the war premium out of oil and DXY softer, every reason to fade the rally just collapsed.
US500.watch: 7,400 is the round-number target; clearance forces momentum funds in. Powell 21:00 TRT — anything other than slightly hawkish is a green light.

headlines.title: McDonald's to report Q1 earnings as value push, viral burger launch may offset consumer softness

Update: Market Chatter: US, Iran Close to Agreement That Could End War, Lead to Broader Deal

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100 Index
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 rocketed 2.08% to a record 28,599 — NVDA's +5.8% session and AMD's strong AI guidance turbocharged chips while Iran de-escalation removed the macro overhang.
NAS100.why: The same AI-spend story that's powered the index for two years is now stacking with a risk-on macro tape. SpaceX/Anthropic Colossus rumours added narrative kerosene.
NAS100.watch: AMZN earnings Thursday after-close — any AWS deceleration spooks the index. Powell at 21:00 TRT is the bigger swing factor.

headlines.title: SpaceX isn't public yet, but Wall Street is already building ETFs for it
Stock Surge on Robust Tech Earnings and US-Iran Peace Hopes
Top Cryptocurrencies Mixed; Bitcoin Holds Above $81,000 Level

US30.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average
US30.past: Dow added 612 points to 49,910 (+1.24%), lagging the cap-weighted indices as energy components dragged on the oil collapse — financials and industrials carried the close.
US30.why: The Dow's energy weighting is its tell here: when oil drops 7% on the week, a price-weighted index will under-perform tech-heavy benchmarks even on a record-close day.
US30.watch: 50,000 is the psychological line; clearance is a free promotional push for retail flows. JPM and GS technicals stay bullish, so leadership remains financials-led.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures edge up on hopes for US-Iran deal
Oil Stabilizes in Wake of Selloff
Dow Jones Futures: Stock Market Hits Highs On Iran-Deal Hopes, Nvidia Leads New Buys; ARM Is Earnings Mover

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin slipped 0.33% to $81,156, under-performing the broad risk-on tape — the geopolitical hedge bid that had powered it from $74k to $82k unwound as Iran tensions eased.
BTC.why: BTC's 2026 narrative is macro hedge for non-Western capital — and that bid softens when the world looks less broken. Two-day ETF outflows of $310M tell the same story.
BTC.watch: $80,000 is the support that decides if this is a 5% wobble or a deeper pullback toward $76k. ETH/BTC compressed sharply — alts may catch a bid first.

headlines.title: Wall Street has a new way to chase the AI memory boom
Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Wednesday, May 6, 2026: Prices up; bitcoin at highest level since January
Bitcoin Miners’ Q1 Losses Mount as AI Pivots Accelerate

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: Ethereum dropped 0.71% to $2,334, again lagging Bitcoin (-0.33%) — the ETH/BTC ratio at 0.029 is now at its lowest since early 2025.
ETH.why: ETH has no clean narrative right now: staking yields below T-bills, L2 fees minimal so token burn is weak, and the institutional story remains BTC-only.
ETH.watch: $2,250 is the line that's held all of April; a break opens $2,000 quickly. The BNY Mellon UAE crypto-custody headline is mildly bullish but not enough to flip the tape.

headlines.title: What is Ethereum? A complete guide to the Ethereum blockchain.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVDA exploded +5.8% to $207.83 — its biggest day in three months — after AMD's data-center beat and SpaceX/Anthropic Colossus capacity rumours reinforced the AI capex peak-far-from-here thesis.
NVDA.why: When AMD beats AND raises, it's fuel for the entire AI complex — they're the second-best vendor and rising tide lifts NVDA's pricing power most. Peak-AI-spend bears just got steamrolled.
NVDA.watch: $215 is the prior high — clearance is a clean breakout. META and GOOGL capex commentary on next-quarter calls is the core bear-case watch.

headlines.title: 'Uber won': Why Instacart might not stand a chance
Tech stocks today: AMD earnings buoy chip stocks, Musk-Altman court battle continues
The space ETFs prepping for liftoff ahead of SpaceX IPO

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: Tesla rose 2.4% to $398.73 on Musk's $55B Texas Terafab chip filing — a strategic pivot positioning Tesla as a vertically-integrated AI/robotaxi play, not just an automaker.
TSLA.why: The Tesla-makes-its-own-chips story is exactly the multi-year capex pitch that re-rates a stock from auto multiples to tech multiples — but only if execution doesn't slip.
TSLA.watch: $400 round-number resistance is the immediate hurdle; above it, momentum funds re-engage. Q2 delivery pre-announcement next week is the binary catalyst.

headlines.title: 'Uber won': Why Instacart might not stand a chance
Elon Musk's SpaceX files initial $55 billion spend for Terafab chip factory in Texas
Is It Too Late To Reassess Tesla (TSLA) After A 40% One Year Run?

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple gained 1.17% to $287.51, climbing with the broad tape and finishing within fractions of an all-time-high close.
AAPL.why: AAPL's catch-up trade has been the AI-laggard pivot — when the rest of MAG7 prints AI numbers, money rotates into Apple's installed-base optionality story for FY2027.
AAPL.watch: $290 is the ATH; a clean break attracts passive index buying. May 21 earnings — Services growth deceleration is the only thing that breaks this story.

headlines.title: Samsung market cap crosses $1 trillion amid memory chip rally
The space ETFs prepping for liftoff ahead of SpaceX IPO

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: MSFT lagged the megacap pack — only +0.63% to $413.96 versus NDX's +2.08% — as cloud-margin concerns kept buyers cautious post-earnings.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the MAG7 name where the AI-spend math is most scrutinised: capex up 60% YoY, Azure growth decelerating, and capacity-constraint excuses wearing thin three quarters running.
MSFT.watch: $410 is critical support; below it, the 200-DMA at $402 comes in fast. Anthropic-Colossus headline is a competitive negative — capacity flowing to a hosted competitor.

headlines.title: Anthropic CEO warns software companies can 'go bust' without AI
Anthropic to rent all AI capacity at SpaceX's Colossus data center

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: META gained 1.31% to $612.88 on the broad risk-on tape; lawsuit overhang from the social-media-addiction case continues to cap relative performance versus GOOGL.
META.why: META's setup is now show-me-earnings-leverage on Reality Labs cuts — that's the only catalyst that re-rates from a 22x P/E. Without it, every legal headline weighs.
META.watch: $620 is the technical pivot. The verdict outcome on the social-media-liability case is the binary — it either kills the overhang or extends it through Q3.

headlines.title: Market Chatter: Meta Platforms Seeks to Overturn Verdict in Social Media Addiction Case

GOOGL.name: Alphabet (Class A)
GOOGL.past: Alphabet outperformed META and MSFT, jumping 2.47% to $398.04 — the AI re-acceleration narrative driven by Gemini-3 hyperscaler wins is the single biggest re-rating catalyst in MAG7.
GOOGL.why: GOOGL is the consensus 2026 catch-up trade: regulatory tail risk receded after April rulings, and Search is no longer leaking to OpenAI as the AI Mode rollout matured.
GOOGL.watch: $400 is the round number that triggers ETF rebalancing. Q2 cloud growth rate is the single number — anything north of 32% YoY locks in the re-rating.

headlines.title: AMD stock surges as the chipmaker forecasts continued AI growth
Anthropic to rent all AI capacity at SpaceX's Colossus data center
What Happens to Nvidia Stock If the AI Build-Out Slows Down? Here's My Answer.

AMZN.name: Amazon.com
AMZN.past: Amazon added 0.53% to $274.99 — under-performing both the SPX and MAG7 average ahead of its earnings tomorrow after-close, classic pre-print de-risking.
AMZN.why: The whole MAG7 tape Friday hinges on AWS growth — consensus +18.5% YoY; a beat to 20%+ keeps the AI rally going, a miss below 17% breaks NVDA et al.
AMZN.watch: AMZN earnings Thursday after-market. Pre-print position-trimming likely caps upside to $278 today; the report is a binary 5-7% overnight move.
